violations.text: The date of the last shellfish served or sold were not marked on the shellfish tags. -The date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. New Violation. Correct By: 09/02/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with a blackish residue. Ice observed in direct contact with black residue. PIC voluntary disposed of ice. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/02/2025

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher in bar area was (0) parts per million (ppm). After PIC primed dishwasher a few times, the chlorine was observed at 50-100 ppm. Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Service was contacted. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/02/2025

(Ahi tuna, sirloin steak and burgers) are served raw or undercooked to the customer's request. Asterisk must be placed on those items to match with the consumer advisory. New Violation. Correct By: 09/02/2025

ROP mussels observed completely thawed out with package intact. Label on package says, "keep frozen". ROP mussels observed at 37 degrees Fahrenheit. Proper thawing of ROP seafood products reviewed. Staff removed ROP mussel from reduce oxygen packaging during this inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/02/2025

Observed insect sticky strips above food prep area in the kitchen and observed insect control devices above the takeout bagging area. Staff removed insect strips during this inspection and will relocate insect control device away from food prep area. Insect control devices shall be installed so that: (1) The devices are not located over a FOOD preparation area; and (2) Dead insects and insect fragments are prevented from being impelled onto or falling on exposed FOOD; clean EQUIPMENT, UTENSILS, and LINENS; and unwrapped SINGLE-SERVICE and SINGLE-USE ARTICLES. New Violation. Correct By: 09/02/2025

Sauces and wet batter were observed held above 41 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods were recently placed into unit/prepped. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/17/2023
The sauce/dressing unit was observed at 48 degrees. Unit was taken out of service. Unit to be serviced prior to use. New Violation. Correct By: 07/17/2023
